title: CLI Token Commands

story.i_want_to: query token cost data from the command line using loopctl cost-summary and loopctl token-report commands
story.so_that: I can check project cost health, agent efficiency, and budget utilization without opening a browser or writing API calls

rationale: The CLI is loopctl's primary interface for human developers and shell scripts. Token analytics endpoints (US-21.4) provide the data, but accessing them requires crafting HTTP requests with auth headers. CLI commands make token data accessible in the same workflow where developers already use loopctl status, loopctl claim, etc. This is especially important for orchestrator scripts that need to make cost-aware decisions in automated pipelines.

acceptance_criteria.description: loopctl cost-summary --project <name|id> displays a project cost overview: total cost (formatted as dollars), total tokens (formatted with K/M suffixes), budget utilization (if budget set), top 3 agents by cost, model mix breakdown. Output is a formatted table by default, --format json for machine-readable output.
loopctl cost-summary --project <name|id> --by-epic breaks down cost per epic: epic name, story count, total cost, avg cost per story, budget utilization. Sorted by total cost descending.
loopctl cost-summary --project <name|id> --by-agent breaks down cost per agent: agent name, stories completed, total cost, avg cost per story, efficiency rank, primary model.
loopctl token-report --story <id> displays detailed token usage for a specific story: list of all token reports with timestamp, model, input/output tokens, cost, phase. Includes totals at the bottom.
loopctl anomalies --project <name|id> lists unresolved cost anomalies: story title, anomaly type, deviation factor, cost vs average. --include-resolved shows all anomalies.

loopctl status --project <name|id> is extended with a cost summary line when token data exists: 'Cost: $X.XX / $Y.YY budget (Z% utilized)' or 'Cost: $X.XX (no budget set)'. This appears alongside existing progress stats.
All CLI cost commands respect the configured API key and tenant. Cost data is only visible for the authenticated tenant.
Cost values are displayed in human-readable format: millicents are converted to dollars with 2 decimal places. Token counts use K/M suffixes (e.g., 150K, 1.2M).

test_cases.name: cost-summary displays project overview
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid API key
Project exists with token usage data across multiple agents and models
expected_results: Output includes total cost in dollars
Output includes token totals with K/M formatting
Output includes top 3 agents ranked by cost
Output includes model mix (percentage per model)

test_cases.name: cost-summary --by-epic shows epic breakdown
preconditions: Project with 3 epics, each with different cost profiles
expected_results: Table with 3 rows, one per epic
Each row shows epic name, story count, total cost, avg cost

test_cases.name: token-report shows per-story detail
preconditions: Story with 3 token usage reports from different models
expected_results: Table with 3 rows showing timestamp, model, input/output tokens, cost
Totals row at bottom summing all values

test_cases.name: JSON output format for scripting
expected_results: JSON structure matches API response format
Parseable by jq or similar tools

test_cases.name: status command includes cost line when data exists
preconditions: Project with token data and a budget set
expected_results: Output includes new cost summary line: 'Cost: $X.XX / $Y.YY budget (Z% utilized)'

test_cases.name: status command omits cost line when no token data

technical_notes: CLI module: Loopctl.CLI.CostCommands (or extend existing command modules).
CLI calls the analytics API endpoints (US-21.4) internally using the configured API key. No direct DB access from CLI.
Table formatting: use the existing CLI table formatter if one exists, or use Erlang's :io.format for column alignment.
The --format json flag should output the raw API response JSON for piping to jq.
Extend the existing loopctl status command to call the project analytics endpoint and append cost data when available.
CLI escript must include the new modules in the escript configuration in mix.exs.
Error handling: if analytics endpoints return empty data, display 'No token data available' rather than an error.
